channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: ECA Digital começa a valer e impõe novas regras para crianças e jovens em redes sociais, jogos e sites
item.description: The Digital ECA, signed by President Lula in September 2024, takes effect today, imposing new rules to protect children and adolescents online. The law bans self-declared age, requires linking accounts of minors under 16 to guardians, mandates age verification in marketplaces and betting platforms, blocks prohibited content, and demands transparency in content moderation from platforms with over one million minor users. Implementation details will be clarified by a decree expected to be published today.

item.title: "Em um país sério, Moraes estaria afastado do cargo", diz Carlos Viana sobre o caso do Banco Master
item.description: Senator Carlos Viana (Podemos-MG), president of the INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPMI), criticized the STF and Minister Alexandre de Moraes, stating that in a serious country, Moraes would be removed from office due to alleged bias in the Banco Master case. He advocated for a CPMI to investigate the bank’s ties to the financial system and accused the Senate of inaction. The remarks were made during the Roda Viva program on TV Cultura on March 16, 2026.

item.title: Caminho para combater o crime é cooperação e não chamar de terrorismo, diz secretário nacional de Segurança
item.description: President Lula will sign the anti-factions bill and decrees to combat organized crime, focusing on financial strangulation of criminal organizations and international cooperation, including with the U.S. National Public Security Secretary Francisco Lucas advocates cooperation as the correct path, contrasting with the Trump administration's threat to label factions like PCC and CV as terrorists. The measure is also supported by right-wing politicians and is expected to be part of Senator Flávio Bolsonaro's platform.

item.title: Governo Lula lança plano para enfrentar crise climática e prevê orçamento de quase R$ 180 bilhões
item.description: President Lula's government launched the National Plan on Climate Change (Plano Clima) with a projected budget of nearly R$ 180 billion by 2025, including R$ 27.5 billion in repayable funds for 2026. Organized around three pillars—mitigation, adaptation, and cross-cutting strategies—the plan aims to eradicate severe food insecurity, protect 4 million people in geohydrological risk zones, make 72.68 million hectares of livestock production sustainable, and increase marine protected areas to 30% by 2035.

item.title: Governo fecha plano de R$ 27,5 bi para redução de gases poluentes
item.description: President Lula’s administration launched the Climate Plan 2024-2035, allocating R$ 27.5 billion for 2026 to cut greenhouse gas emissions and achieve net-zero by 2050. The Climate Fund, managed by BNDES and MMA, relies on repayable loans and includes five cross-cutting strategies such as just transition and transparency. Environmentalists, however, criticized the plan for allowing a 1% increase in emissions from agribusiness.

item.title: PT anuncia lançamento da pré-candidatura de Haddad ao governo de SP na sexta
item.description: The PT is set to announce Fernando Haddad as its pre-candidate for governor of São Paulo on Friday (20), in an event featuring President Lula and ministers at the São Paulo Chemical Workers’ Union. Prior to the announcement, Haddad will attend the 17th Federative Caravan (March 19-20) and a tribute to former Uruguayan president Pepe Mujica at UFABC. A Datafolha poll shows Tarcísio de Freitas leading with 44% voting intention compared to Haddad’s 31% in a first-round scenario.

item.title: Deputado do Podemos entra na disputa por vaga no TCU, que já tem cinco candidatos
item.description: Federal Deputy Gilson Daniel (Podemos-ES) officially announced his candidacy for the vacant position at the TCU following the compulsory retirement of Minister Aroldo Cedraz in February 2026. The race already includes five other formally registered candidates. Appointment to the TCU is made by the National Congress upon presidential nomination, subject to Senate approval.

item.title: Valdemar fala sobre vice de Flávio, prisão de Bolsonaro e fim da 6x1
item.description: Valdemar Costa Neto, president of the PL party, announced that Senator Tereza Cristina (PP-MS) will be the vice-presidential candidate on Flávio Bolsonaro’s (PL-RJ) ticket for the 2026 elections. He also commented on Jair Bolsonaro’s legal situation and the end of the 6x1 work schedule, emphasizing the need for labor reforms.

item.title: Haja, coração: em campo, na Copa de 2026, a pior seleção brasileira da história
item.description: The Brazilian national team is experiencing its worst historical moment ahead of the 2026 World Cup, with a squad deemed weak across all positions — especially midfield (Casemiro, Andrey, Fabinho) and attack (Mateus Cunha, Igor Thiago, João Pedro) — despite widespread support for Italian coach Carlo Ancelotti. The article contrasts this lineup with past generations, emphasizing the lack of true stars and the reliance on a miracle, such as Neymar’s potential return, to avoid failure. Remaining hope rests on the 'supernatural of Almeida,' a concept from Nelson Rodrigues, implying only footballing fate can save the team.

item.title: Bolívia quer usar fronteira com 5 países para distribuir energia
item.description: Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz announced that Bolivia aims to become an energy distribution hub, leveraging its borders with Brazil, Chile, Peru, Paraguay, and Argentina through a new hydrocarbons law and energy exchange agreements with Brazil. The initiative seeks to boost Bolivia’s economic growth and meet regional energy demand, emphasizing cross-border cooperation and local development. In a meeting with Lula, both leaders signed pacts on security, including combating human trafficking, drug trafficking, money laundering, and environmental crimes.

item.title: Neymar tem até 16 jogos para convencer Ancelotti a levá-lo à Copa do Mundo de 2026
item.description: Neymar has 16 matches with Santos until May 18 to prove he is physically fit for the 2026 World Cup, after being omitted from friendlies against France and Croatia due to not being at 100%, according to Ancelotti. The CBF will submit a preliminary list of 55 names to FIFA on May 11, with the final list released on May 18. Despite the tight schedule, it is unlikely Neymar will play all matches due to injury prevention efforts, having already been rested in recent games.

item.title: Careca do INSS pagou viagem de Lulinha a Portugal, admite defesa ao STF
item.description: The defense of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, known as Lulinha, admitted that lobbyist Antonio Carlos Camilo Antunes, nicknamed 'Careca do INSS', paid for a trip he took to Portugal. The admission was made in the context of a case before the Supreme Federal Court (STF).

item.title: Edinho Silva diz que PT é antissistema e pede mobilização eleitoral
item.description: Edinho Silva, president of the Workers' Party (PT), stated that the party is 'anti-system' and the party of change, calling for mobilization in the streets and on social media to show strength. His remarks, made in an Instagram video, reinforce the party's discourse ahead of the 2026 elections, as preparations advance for Fernando Haddad's candidacy for governor of São Paulo.

item.title: Bolívia classifica crime organizado como terrorismo e firma pacto com Brasil
item.description: Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz classified criminal organizations such as the PCC and CV as terrorism, aligning with the U.S. proposal, during a meeting with Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva in Brasília. He also announced a cooperation pact between Bolivia and Brazil to combat transnational organized crime.

item.title: Lulinha admite ao STF que teve viagem a Portugal bancada pelo Careca do INSS: ‘Relação esporádica’
item.description: The defense of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, the eldest son of President Lula, admitted to the STF that he took a trip to Portugal in November 2024 funded by Antônio Camilo Antunes, the 'Careca do INSS', who is under suspicion of embezzling pension funds. Lulinha claims the relationship was purely social and occasional, with no commercial ties or knowledge of wrongdoing, and that he accepted the invitation to learn about a medicinal cannabidiol project due to his niece's medical needs.

item.title: Investigaçőes contradizem explicaçőes de Javier Milei sobre escândalo com criptomoeda
item.description: An investigation by Argentina’s Public Ministry uncovered documents on the phone of businessman Mauricio Novelli suggesting a $5 million deal between Javier Milei and Hayden Davis, CEO of Kelsier Ventures, to promote the cryptocurrency $Libra. The findings contradict Milei’s claims that he had no close ties to those involved and promoted the asset only as a private individual, indicating direct negotiation with his circle, including a payment to his sister, Karina Milei. The Argentine Presidency reiterated it would not engage with journalistic versions, stating Milei had already explained himself publicly.

item.title: Papo Antagonista – Daniel Vorcaro surta e fará delação premiada
item.description: On Papo Antagonista, Daniel Vorcaro erupted and confirmed he signed a plea bargain deal, sparking political repercussions. The show also covered Jair Bolsonaro’s health, the legal victory of student Isadora Borges over Erika Hilton, a live rebuttal of Guilherme Boulos, and Cuba’s demographic situation with the return of part of its population.

item.title: Número de Nikolas aparece entre os contatos de Vorcaro
item.description: The INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee found the phone numbers of federal deputy Nikolas Ferreira (PL-MG) and Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) among the contacts of banker Daniel Vorcado. Nikolas stated he does not recall communicating with Vorcado via phone or messages, though he acknowledged such contact is 'possible'.

item.title: De olho em cenário pós-eleitoral em SP, PL ‘rouba’ mais um deputado estadual do PSD
item.description: The PL intensified its strategy to expand its bench in the São Paulo State Assembly (Alesp) by welcoming state deputy Oséias de Madureira (ex-PSD), federal deputy Cezinha de Madureira, pastors Rogério Martins and Elias Amaral, and state deputy Marcelo Aguiar (ex-Podemos). The move aims to consolidate the PL as the largest parliamentary bloc in Alesp by 2026 to strengthen its position in the race to succeed André do Prado as president of the legislature.

item.title: Presidente da Bolívia associa narcotráfico a “terrorismo”
item.description: Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz linked narcotrafficking activities to terrorism in an interview following a meeting with Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva at Itamaraty. He emphasized the arrest of Uruguayan narcotrafficker Sebastián Marset, an ally of the PCC, as a key step in reducing violence and instability in Bolivia, particularly in Santa Cruz de la Sierra. Paz stated that Bolivia faces the export of violence from Brazil and announced a cooperation agreement to combat transnational organized crime, including drug trafficking, money laundering, and cybercrimes.

item.title: A disputa pelo Senado no Amazonas, segundo a Quaest
item.description: Senator Eduardo Braga (MDB-AM) leads the Senate race in Amazonas with 28% voting intentions, followed by Capitão Alberto Neto (PL) at 19%, according to a Quaest poll conducted between March 5 and 11. Marcos Rotta (Avante) and Plínio Valério (PSD) are tied at 11%, while Marcelo Ramos (PT) stands at 10%. Undecided voters account for 6%, and blank/null/not voting for 15%.

item.title: O favoritismo de Aziz na disputa pelo governo do Amazonas, segundo a Quaest
item.description: The Quaest poll shows Omar Aziz (PSD) as the frontrunner for the Amazonas governorship in 2026, with 33% of voting intentions in the first round, followed by David Almeida (Avante) at 23% and Maria do Carmo Seffair (PL) at 21%. In the second-round scenarios, Aziz leads comfortably with 47% against Seffair’s 31% or 43% against Almeida’s 31%.

item.title: Aposentadoria compulsória de juízes é considerada inconstitucional pelo Supremo
item.description: Supreme Court Minister Flávio Dino declared the compulsory retirement of judges unconstitutional, overturning the conviction of a Rio de Janeiro judge and ordering a new trial without that penalty. The decision could trigger a chain reaction, as compulsorily retired judges currently retain income and privileges despite serious misconduct. The text also covers the administrative process against Eduardo Bolsonaro in the Federal Police, Jair Bolsonaro’s hospitalization for pneumonia, Trump’s criticism of Middle East responses, and threats by Iran’s Revolutionary Guard against U.S. companies.

item.title: Brasil exporta violência para Bolívia, diz Rodrigo Paz
item.description: Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz claimed that Brazil exports violence to Bolivia, while praising the frankness of his bilateral meeting with Lula and announcing cooperation agreements on transnational organized crime, including drug trafficking, money laundering, and environmental crimes. He also highlighted the arrest of Uruguayan narcotrafficker Sebastián Marset, linked to the PCC, as a key step in combating organized crime.

item.title: Flávio diz que digital vai ser ‘90% da campanha’ e pede união da direita para vencer eleições
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) called for unity on the right around his 2026 presidential pre-candidacy during a virtual meeting with Bolsonarist militants, stating that 90% of his campaign will be digital. He emphasized increasing the PL’s congressional caucus as more important than winning governorships and urged supporters to stop attacking each other online, defending federal deputy Gustavo Gayer as an ally whose loyalty should be respected despite internal tensions with factions aligned with Nikolas Ferreira, Tarcísio de Freitas, and Michelle Bolsonaro.

item.title: Lula assina nesta terça (17) decreto com regras para conteúdos proibidos a crianças na internet
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva will sign on Tuesday (17) decrees regulating the ECA Digital (Digital Statute for Children and Adolescents), establishing rules to prohibit minors' access to harmful online content, including weapons, illegal gambling, and casinos with reward systems. National Secretary of Digital Rights Victor Fernandes said the decrees will provide operationality to the statute’s rules, though some provisions, such as age verification, still require regulation by the ANPD and may take months to implement.

item.title: PT aciona TSE contra Flávio por vídeos que associam Lula ao crime organizado
item.description: The Workers' Party (PT) has filed a request with the TSE to remove three videos posted by Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) that associate President Lula with organized crime. The PT claims the content is false and constitutes an electoral offense. The action aims to curb the spread of disinformation on social media.

item.title: Marina diz estar “tranquila” sobre não disputar Senado em SP
item.description: Environment Minister Marina Silva (Rede) said she is 'tranquil' about not running for the Senate in São Paulo, emphasizing her focus on the 'civilizational coefficient' and mentioning Simone Tebet, Fernando Haddad, and Márcio França as potential candidates. She stated she may or may not run, as debate continues in the state. Tebet’s confirmation to run reduced open slots in the alliance led by Haddad, who will announce his gubernatorial pre-candidacy on March 19.

item.title: Leite, Ratinho Jr. e Caiado defendem reforma no Supremo
item.description: Governors Eduardo Leite (RS), Ratinho Júnior (PR), and Ronaldo Caiado (GO), all from the PSD and 2026 presidential pre-candidates, defended a reform of the Supreme Federal Court's composition in a joint interview on TV Bandeirantes' Canal Livre. They also criticized a proposed constitutional amendment that would abolish the 44-hour weekly workweek. The remarks were made during a discussion on institutions and labor rights.

item.title: Advogado admite viagem de Lulinha a Portugal com Careca do INSS
item.description: Lawyer Marco Aurélio de Carvalho confirmed that Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, known as Lulinha, traveled to Portugal in November accompanied by lobbyist Antônio Carlos Camilo Antunes, nicknamed 'Careca do INSS'. The statement was made on Monday, October 16, raising concerns about potential conflicts of interest and influence peddling by relatives of public officials.

item.title: Advogado nega que Lulinha seja 'filho do rapaz' citado em investigação
item.description: The lawyer for Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, Marco Aurélio de Carvalho, denied in an interview with GloboNews that the son of President Lula is the 'son of the guy' mentioned in the Federal Police investigations regarding the case. The statement aims to clarify allegations raised in the PF probe, reinforcing the defense of Fábio Lula. The lawyer did not specify who the 'guy' referred to, but ruled out any filial relationship between that individual and the president's son.

item.title: Tarcísio reestrutura setor anticorrupção da Polícia Civil
item.description: São Paulo’s government, led by Tarcísio de Freitas, restructured the anticorruption unit of the Civil Police, establishing the Integrity Management Unit (UGI) with nine appointed officials. The measure, published in the State Official Gazette, aims to strengthen efforts to combat corruption within the state police forces. The initiative comes amid growing public demand for greater transparency and accountability in public security institutions.

item.title: Qualquer acordo de delação terá que passar pelo filtro do clube do uísque de Vorcaro
item.description: Daniel Vorcaro, a banker accused of obstructing justice and leader of an active militia, may cooperate with authorities despite his power. His plea deal would require STF homologation and PGR assessment, emphasizing the influence of figures like Paulo Gonet and Andrei Rodrigues, who reportedly socialized with him. The text suggests his connections could facilitate or distort the cooperation process.

item.title: CPI do INSS encontra contato de Flávio Bolsonaro e Nikolas em celular de Vorcaro
item.description: The INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Commission found contacts of Flávio Bolsonaro and Nikolas Ferreira on the cellphone of former employee Vorcaro, raising suspicions of links between lawmakers and pension fraud schemes. The discovery intensifies investigations into possible illegal favors within the INSS benefits system.

item.title: Cinco pontos-chave sobre a França após o 1º turno das eleições municipais
item.description: The first round of municipal elections in France revealed significant advances by the far-right and far-left, signaling strong political polarization one year ahead of the 2027 presidential election. Jordan Bardella of the National Rally (RN) leads in polls, while traditional parties like The Republicans struggle to remain relevant. High abstention (around 43%) reflects voter discontent amid an ongoing political crisis since the 2024 legislative elections.

item.title: ‘Plano Clima’ é lançado após meses de atrito com o agronegócio
item.description: The Lula government launched the Climate Plan, aiming to reduce Brazil's greenhouse gas emissions by 49% to 58% by 2035, using 2022 as the baseline. The announcement follows months of tension with the agribusiness sector, which fears impacts on production and competitiveness. The plan outlines directives to fulfill Brazil's commitment under the Paris Agreement.

item.title: Ex-ministro de Bolsonaro processa deputado do PL
item.description: Former Bolsonaro minister Gilson Machado Neto has sued federal deputy Coronel Meira (PL) over allegations that he 'sold' former President Jair Bolsonaro for over 10 million reais, allegedly tied to a forró band contract with the Pernambuco government. Meira also suggested that STF Minister Alexandre de Moraes lifted precautionary measures against Gilson after he left the PL. The case has been assigned to STF Minister Gilmar Mendes, who has not yet ruled.

item.title: Brasil quer aumentar produção e importação de gás da Bolívia
item.description: President Lula stated that Brazil aims to increase the production and import of natural gas from Bolivia, emphasizing energy partnership as a cornerstone of bilateral cooperation. During the visit of Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz to the Planalto Palace, both countries signed agreements to expand gas exchange and interconnect their electrical systems, focusing on energy security and regional integration.

item.title: Caiado se adianta a governo federal e vai assinar acordo de minerais críticos com entre Goiás e EUA
item.description: Governor of Goiás, Ronaldo Caiado (PSD), will sign a memorandum of understanding with the Donald Trump administration on critical minerals next Wednesday (18). The state hosts Brazil’s only operating rare earths miner and is seen as essential to the country’s mineral policy.

item.title: Lógica do Estado-cliente embasa política externa de Flávio Bolsonaro
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) has realistic chances of winning the 2026 election, tied with Lula in second-round simulations. His foreign policy, grounded in the 'Zema Doctrine,' would advocate for automatic alignment of Brazilian interests with those of the U.S. under Trump, turning Brazil into a client state of Washington, even at the cost of multilateralism and other trade relationships. Analysts warn that, despite ideological rhetoric, pressures from the productive sector — especially agribusiness — may constrain extremes in the foreign policy of a potential Bolsonaro son administration.

item.title: O copia e cola governista na CPI do Crime Organizado para atacar adversários do PT
item.description: Senators Humberto Costa (PT-PE) and Soraya Thronicke (Podemos-MS) submitted identical requests in the Organized Crime CPI, using copied text to attack PT opponents amid Lula’s declining poll numbers. The move reveals a government strategy to instrumentalize the CPI for electoral purposes, targeting opposition figures ahead of the 2026 elections.

item.title: Paes e Castro travam 'guerra dos ônibus' em semana de virada de embate eleitoral
item.description: Rio de Janeiro Mayor Eduardo Paes (PSD) carried out deliveries in areas typically under state jurisdiction during his final week in office, laying groundwork for a potential run for the Guanabara Palace. This move intensified an electoral clash with the state government led by Governor Claudio Castro (PL), which retaliated with criticism, sparking what has been dubbed the 'bus war' due to disputes over public transportation responsibilities.

item.title: Haddad deve deixar Ministério da Fazenda na próxima sexta-feira (20)
item.description: Finance Minister Fernando Haddad is expected to leave office next Friday (20), with the publication of his dismissal. He plans to take a ten-day break before officially launching his pre-campaign for governor of São Paulo. The move signals his focus on the 2026 state election.

item.title: Cinco pontos-chave sobre a França após 1º turno de eleições municipais
item.description: The first round of municipal elections in France revealed a scenario of extreme polarization, with significant advances by the far-right (RN) and the radical left, reflecting the political crisis triggered by the 2024 legislative elections. Despite high abstention (around 43%), the RN consolidated its presence in over 60 municipalities, leading in cities like Perpignan, Toulon, and Nice, and could contest Marseille in the runoff. The results indicate that the 2027 presidential race will be decided by alliances between the left, center-right, and far-right, as Emmanuel Macron cannot run for re-election.

item.title: STF mantém limite de candidatos por partido nas eleições proporcionais
item.description: The Supreme Federal Court upheld the limit on the number of candidates parties can register in proportional elections, confirming the constitutionality of the rule allowing one more candidate than available seats. The court rejected the Cidadania party's claim that legislative changes to Law 14.211/2021 violated due process, ruling they were mere formatting corrections. Bolsonaro's vetoes on exceptions for small states and municipalities remain in effect.

item.title: Gleisi Hoffmann processa Flávio Bolsonaro por vídeo em que é associada a organizações criminosas
item.description: Minister Gleisi Hoffmann sued Senator Flávio Bolsonaro over a video associating her with criminal organizations, claiming abusive use of free speech for defamation. The AI-generated video links the PT to organized crime and references a controversial Lula statement about drug traffickers. Hoffmann seeks moral damages and removal of the content.

item.title: A aposta de Caiado para crescer nas pesquisas e vencer disputa no PSD
item.description: PSD president Gilberto Kassab must choose the party’s 2026 presidential candidate by end of March among Ronaldo Caiado, Ratinho Júnior and Eduardo Leite. Caiado is betting on conservative themes — such as supporting amnesty for January 8 protesters — to boost his poll visibility and surpass internal rivals, especially Ratinho Júnior, who currently leads internal preferences. However, this strategy risks placing Caiado in the same political space as Flávio Bolsonaro, making it difficult to build a distinct alternative on the right.

item.title: Lula defende parceria com a Bolívia em diferentes setores; veja principais
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva met with Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz at the Planalto Palace to discuss bilateral partnerships. He highlighted Brazil as Bolivia’s second-largest trading partner and advocated for strengthened cooperation in agriculture, livestock, biotechnology, and river infrastructure, including a tripartite project with Bolivia and Paraguay to improve the navigability of the Paraguay River waterway and the Tamengo Canal.

item.title: Lula deixa novo projeto do fim da escala 6x1 em stand-by e quer esperar avanço de PEC na Câmara
item.description: President Lula has decided to put on hold a new bill proposing the end of the 6x1 work scale, choosing instead to wait for the progress of a constitutional amendment (PEC) currently under debate in the Chamber of Deputés. After consulting with ministers and allies, he prioritized the constitutional route to avoid legislative duplication.

item.title: Lula cita conflitos que atrapalham fornecimento de combustíveis e diz que Bolívia é fonte segura de gás
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva stated that global conflicts threaten fuel supplies, but emphasized that Bolivia is a secure source of natural gas for Brazil. He made the remark alongside Bolivian President Rodrigo Paz during Paz's official visit to Brazil.
